Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC1 - Database Setup and Prerequisites
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite Setup - Restore Test Database: On VM with test build: restore and upgrade the linkages database that is located here: \QASTORE01\Database Backups\Integrity Datasets_Databases\REGRESSION TEST DATABASES\7.3.1 \Topology Linkages
Step Expected: Database is successfully restored on test VM

Step Action: Prerequisite Setup - Restore Release Database: On one of the Release Build VMs (IAHcVPPASSQLQA2 or IAHcVPPASISQA09) restore and connect to a release version of the same database
Step Expected: Database is successfully restored on release VM

Step Action: Delete and Re-add Topology Asset: Due to bug fix in 8.0.0.63, delete the topology asset and re-add via Admin Utility Asset Configuration
Step Expected: Topology asset is successfully deleted and re-added

Step Action: Create New Topology Import Schedule: Add a new Topology import schedule in Admin Utility
Step Expected: Schedule is created successfully

Step Action: Run Topology Import Schedule: Execute the new Topology import schedule
Step Expected: Schedule runs without errors

Step Action: Summary: Database setup completed successfully for both test and release VMs with topology asset configured and imported
Step Expected: Both environments are ready for topology testing with verified data

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC2 - Basic Map Display and Zoom Functionality
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC1 - Database Setup and Prerequisites has been completed successfully
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met

Step Action: Navigate to Topology Page: On both VMs (test and release), go to Cyber page > Topology
Step Expected: A series of nodes representing available PCs, network switches, firewall, and Asset Discovery Assets are displayed. The nodes are the same on both VMs. Scroll bars are added to cover the larger real estate

Step Action: Test Pink Windows Overview Box: Go to the pink Windows Overview box in lower left corner and drag it around
Step Expected: Dragging this box allows you to navigate across the larger screen

Step Action: Test Zoom to Fit: Click Zoom to Fit icon at the top
Step Expected: The entire map zooms out to fit on the screen

Step Action: Test Zoom In: Click Zoom in icon at the top
Step Expected: The screen size expands and zooms in again

Step Action: Summary: Basic map display and zoom controls verified to function correctly on both test and release VMs
Step Expected: Map display and navigation tools work as expected with consistent behavior across environments

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC3 - Perspective Editor - Inventory Display
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC2 - Basic Map Display and Zoom Functionality has been completed successfully
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met

Step Action: Open Perspective Editor: On both VMs, click in the Perspective Editor drop down
Step Expected: Perspective Editor dropdown opens

Step Action: Select All Inventory Checkboxes: Select all the checkboxes under inventory and Apply
Step Expected: Checkboxes are selected

Step Action: Collapse and Zoom In: Collapse the Perspective Editor and click Zoom in icon repeatedly
Step Expected: The Inventory items applicable to each node are displayed and match on both VMs. Navigating around the screen displays applicable inventory items for each node for Recon and Network devices

Step Action: Clear Selections: In Perspective Editor drop down, click the icon at the top to Select None
Step Expected: All selections are cleared

Step Action: Summary: Inventory information displays correctly in Perspective Editor with consistent results across test and release VMs
Step Expected: Perspective Editor inventory display functionality verified

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC4 - Perspective Editor - Vulnerabilities and Patches
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC3 - Perspective Editor - Inventory Display has been completed successfully
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met

Step Action: Configure Vulnerability and Patch Display: In the Perspective Editor drop down, expand Vulnerabilities and Patches, click the checkboxes for Severity and Status and apply
Step Expected: Checkboxes are selected

Step Action: Zoom In to View Details: Zoom in on the map
Step Expected: The inventory items are removed from the Nodes and Vulnerability and Patch information is displayed with severities populated (see example screenshot). The display is the same on both VMs

Step Action: Add MAC Address: In perspective editor add inventory item for MAC Address and apply
Step Expected: The Mac address is displayed by the recon nodes as well as Vulnerability and patch information

Step Action: Summary: Vulnerability, patch, and MAC address information displays correctly through Perspective Editor
Step Expected: Perspective Editor vulnerability and patch display functionality verified with consistent results

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC5 - Save and Load Custom Perspectives
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC4 - Perspective Editor - Vulnerabilities and Patches has been completed with custom settings applied
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met with custom perspective configured

Step Action: Test Disabled Delete Button: Hover the Trash can icon button beside the Select a Perspective drop down
Step Expected: The Trash icon button shows a disabled hover image. The hover text is 'Delete perspective'

Step Action: Open Save Perspective Dialog: Click the save icon on the tool bar next to the Select a Perspective field
Step Expected: There is a popup Save Perspective to save the current perspective selection

Step Action: Save Custom Perspective: Enter a name and Save
Step Expected: Perspective is saved successfully

Step Action: Reset Page: Navigate to Topology from left Nav to reset the page to its original display and zoom in
Step Expected: Only the default IP address is displayed alongside each Recon and Network devices node

Step Action: Load Saved Perspective: Select the new addition in Select a Perspective drop down and expand the Perspective drop down
Step Expected: The selections made for the saved perspective are checked. The trashcan icon button next to Select a Perspective drop down is orange and enabled

Step Action: Verify Display: Zoom in
Step Expected: The selected inventory item, VM and patch information is displayed for the recon nodes. The Perspective editor drop down has the saved items in the list checked

Step Action: Summary: Custom perspectives can be saved, loaded, and display correct configuration with delete functionality enabled
Step Expected: Perspective save and load functionality verified successfully

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC6 - Navigation Menu Display and Filtering
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC5 - Save and Load Custom Perspectives has been completed successfully
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met

Step Action: Enable Navigation Menu: On both VMs zoom out to see more of the nodes. Click the filter icon in tool bar and in the Map Display Filters popup enable the Navigation menu. Then close the Map Display Filters popup
Step Expected: A navigation popup is displayed listing all the Workstation nodes, LLDP Discovery nodes, Asset Discovery, DCS, Firewalls, Routers and Switches. The list is the same in both VMs Navigation menus

Step Action: Test Navigation to Node: In the Navigation menu, click on a Recon Node
Step Expected: The map navigates you to that node (you may have to move the Navigation menu to see it)

Step Action: Test Dynamic Menu Update: On both VMs with the Navigation menu open, use the Navigation menu to locate the DCS node and expand the DCS node down to several sub level nodes
Step Expected: The Navigation menu updates on the fly to include all the DCS sub nodes and associated items currently visible in the map. The expanded list is the same on both VMs

Step Action: Navigate to Sub-Node: In the Navigation menu click on one of the sub nodes now visible under the DCS
Step Expected: You are navigated in the map to that node and it is highlighted in blue (see screenshot example)

Step Action: Summary: Navigation menu displays all asset types correctly and allows successful navigation to specific nodes with dynamic updates
Step Expected: Navigation menu functionality verified with consistent behavior across VMs

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC7 - Navigation Menu Search Functionality
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC6 - Navigation Menu Display and Filtering has been completed with Navigation menu open
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met with Navigation menu displayed

Step Action: Test Single Word Search: In the Navigation menu use search field at the top of the popup to enter a single word to filter on one or more items in the list and click the refresh icon button below the search field
Step Expected: The Navigation list returns the results of that search and the button icon has changed to a filter. NOTE: See Summary for why this doesn't always work!

Step Action: Test OR Search: In the Navigation menu scroll to the top. Click the filter icon button below search to clear the filter. Enter two search words represented in the Navigation list and select the word OR and click the Refresh icon button
Step Expected: The Navigation list returns the results of the OR filter

Step Action: Test AND Search: Click the filter icon button below search to clear the filter. Enter two search words represented in the Navigation list, select the word AND and click the Refresh icon button
Step Expected: The Navigation list returns the results of the AND filter

Step Action: Summary: Navigation menu search functionality verified with single word, OR, and AND filters working correctly
Step Expected: Navigation menu search and filtering capabilities function as expected

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC8 - Hide and Show Nodes
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC6 - Navigation Menu Display and Filtering has been completed successfully
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met

Step Action: Prepare to Hide Node: Minimize the Navigation menu and zoom out. Right click on any Node (get a screenshot or note its name)
Step Expected: Context menu appears

Step Action: Hide Node: In the dial popup click Hide/Show
Step Expected: The node is hidden

Step Action: Enable Hidden Nodes Display: Click the Filter icon and in Map Display Filter enable Hidden Nodes. Close the Map Display Filter popup
Step Expected: The Node is displayed again, with a blue border but it is grayed out to indicate it is hidden

Step Action: Unhide Node: Right click in the hidden node again and in the dial popup click Hide/Show
Step Expected: The node is displayed again in normal coloration

Step Action: Summary: Hide and unhide functionality verified with proper visual indicators for hidden nodes
Step Expected: Node visibility controls work correctly with appropriate visual feedback

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC9 - Create and Load Favorites
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC5 - Save and Load Custom Perspectives has been completed with a saved perspective selected
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met with custom perspective active

Step Action: Set Up View for Favorite: Ensure the Perspective that you saved is selected. Zoom in enough on one or 2 nodes to see the Perspective Editor info. Take a screenshot
Step Expected: View is configured for saving

Step Action: Open Save Favorite Dialog: Click the save button next to Favorites field
Step Expected: There is a popup to enter a Favorites name

Step Action: Save Favorite: Enter a name for the Favorite
Step Expected: The Favorites drop down includes your addition

Step Action: Reset Page: Refresh the web page and return to topology
Step Expected: The map is displayed expanded but neither the perspective nor Favorite is selected, the map is zoomed out

Step Action: Load Saved Favorite: In Favorites drop down select your favorite
Step Expected: The view changes to display just the location and zoom level and perspective information that matches your screenshot from previous step

Step Action: Summary: Favorites can be created and loaded successfully, restoring exact view configuration including perspective, zoom, and location
Step Expected: Favorites functionality verified to preserve and restore complete view state

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC10 - Update and Manage Favorites
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC9 - Create and Load Favorites has been completed with a favorite created and loaded
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met with existing favorite

Step Action: Modify Favorite: Make another change to your favorite view (zoom or perspective change)
Step Expected: Changes are made to the current view

Step Action: Update Favorite: Click Save button and click OK to the popup to override
Step Expected: Favorite is updated

Step Action: Test Favorite Persistence: Change to a sub location and then return to the same location you were in when you saved the favorite. Select the favorite again
Step Expected: Your latest changes are saved and restored correctly

Step Action: Summary: Favorites can be updated and changes persist correctly, with override functionality working as expected
Step Expected: Favorite update and persistence functionality verified successfully

Title: ISS\Cyber Integrity\Topology\TC11 - Delete Custom Perspective
Step Expected: Integrity\_Aha-HexagonPPM\Cyber\Corporate

Step Action: Prerequisite: TC5 - Save and Load Custom Perspectives has been completed with at least one custom perspective saved
Step Expected: Prerequisites are met with custom perspective available

Step Action: Select Perspective for Deletion: Ensure the Perspective you created is selected
Step Expected: Custom perspective is selected

Step Action: Initiate Delete: To the right of Select a perspective drop down click the trash can button
Step Expected: A popup is displayed for you to confirm the deletion

Step Action: Confirm Deletion: Confirm and delete the Perspective
Step Expected: The Trashcan button is disabled. The map doesn't change its display

Step Action: Verify Deletion: Click the Select a Perspective drop down
Step Expected: It displays 'NO DATA FOUND'

Step Action: Summary: Custom perspectives can be successfully deleted with proper confirmation and UI state updates
Step Expected: Perspective deletion functionality verified with correct cleanup of UI elements
